meta.title: Problems with ICS Proprietary Encryption (or Encraption)
meta.description:
If you hear that a radio or ICS system that you are considering uses proprietary encryption, run away (or do a lot of due diligence). It is hard enough to implement vetted crypto primitives and protocols correctly. It is even harder for a vendor to invent their own that is any good.

At 5:15 of this short S4xEurope video, Vladimir Dashchenko of Kaspersky Lab goes over three different proprietary encryption algorithms used in ICS. All have serious problems that would lead the asset owner / user to have a false sense of confidentiality provided by the encraption.
